case_title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF THE CALIFORNIA VS. THE NORTH RIVER INSURANCE CO.

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE SUMMARY JUDGMENT, TO VACATE FORFEITURE AND EXONERATE BOND;, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT THE NORTH RIVER INSURANCE CO. BAD BOYS BAIL BONDS HEARING SET FOR APR-23-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF SURREPLY IN OPPOS FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF THE CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT THE NORTH RIVER INSURANCE CO. & BAD BOYS BAIL BONDS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE SUMMARY JUDGMENT, TO VACATE FORFEITURE AND EXONERATE BOND IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. PRIOR TO THE HEARING, THE COURT SIGNED A STIPULATED ORDER VACATING THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
OPPOSITION TO MTN BY REAL PARTY IN INTEREST TO VACATE SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF THE CALIFORNIA
SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF ATTORNEY JEFFERSON T. STAMP FILED BY DEFENDANT THE NORTH RIVER INSURANCE CO. BAD BOYS BAIL BONDS
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-23-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF THE CALIFORNIA AS TO DEFENDANT THE NORTH RIVER INSURANCE CO. JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED

UNDER APPLICATION TO THE CLERK FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF THE CALIFORNIA RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT THE NORTH RIVER INSURANCE CO. $70,000.00 PRINCIPAL, $450.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $70,450.00, SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT
REPLY MEMORANDUM FILED BY DEFENDANT THE NORTH RIVER INSURANCE CO. BAD BOYS BAIL BONDS

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS THE NORTH RIVER INSURANCE COMPANY AND BAD BOYS BAIL BONDS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE SUMMARY JUDGMENT, TO VACATE FORFEITURE AND EXONERATE BOND IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO MAY 23, 2013 TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN APRIL 23 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
SURREPLY IN OPPOS TO MOTION BY REAL PARTY IN INTEREST FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF THE CALIFORNIA
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-23-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY DEFENDANT THE NORTH RIVER INSURANCE CO. BAD BOYS BAIL BONDS

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment, To Vacate Forfeiture And Exonerate Bond;
Off Calendar May-23-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court vacated its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE NORTH RIVER INSURANCE CO. & BAD BOYS BAIL BONDS' Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment, To Vacate Forfeiture And Exonerate Bond is ordered off calendar. Prior to the hearing, the Court signed a stipulated order vacating the summary judgment. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment, To Vacate Forfeiture And Exonerate Bond;
Off Calendar Apr-23-2013 Continued To May-23-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS THE NORTH RIVER INSURANCE COMPANY AND BAD BOYS BAIL BONDS' Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment, To Vacate Forfeiture And Exonerate Bond is continued on the court's own motion to May 23, 2013 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 23 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
